title: Spreadsheets, Python, Reproducibility, and Calculation Checks · Unfamiliar Decision Lab
summary: Turn a hand calculation into a reusable, tested worksheet and small Python model. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a reproducible calculation tool with test cases with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use relative and absolute cell references intentionally in copied formulas.
Add input constraints, units, labels, and audit cells to a spreadsheet model.
Express a unit-aware calculation as readable Python variables, functions, and output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Reproducible Report for a reproducible calculation tool with test cases.
steps.why: The Reproducible Report result for a reproducible calculation tool with test cases is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.
